overview: This specimen appears to be a type of stinkhorn fungus. It typically starts as a white, egg-shaped structure before developing its distinct shape. The head often produces a sticky, foul-smelling slime designed to attract insects. These fungi are commonly associated with rotting wood buried in soil or mulch.
